article.title: Microsoft April 2026 Patch Tuesday Fixes 167 Flaws, Including an Actively Exploited SharePoint Zero-Day and a Defender Privilege Escalation
article.summary: Microsoft shipped its second-largest monthly security release on record, patching a SharePoint spoofing flaw already being exploited and a Defender elevation-of-privilege bug with public exploit code.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Microsoft released its April 2026 Patch Tuesday update on April 14, fixing 167 security vulnerabilities across Windows, Office, SharePoint, Defender, and related products. According to [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-april-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-167-flaws-2-zero-days/), the release addresses two zero-day vulnerabilities: one actively exploited in the wild, and one publicly disclosed with exploit code already circulating.

The drop is unusually large. Satnam Narang of Tenable told [Krebs on Security](https://krebsonsecurity.com/2026/04/patch-tuesday-april-2026-edition/) that April marks "the second-biggest Patch Tuesday ever for Microsoft," a characterization echoed by Trend Micro's Zero Day Initiative, whose head of threat awareness Dustin Childs called it "the second-largest monthly release in Microsoft's history" in comments to [CyberScoop](https://cyberscoop.com/microsoft-patch-tuesday-april-2026/). Childs attributed part of the surge to AI-assisted vulnerability research, noting that ZDI's "incoming rate has essentially tripled, making triage a challenge," as reported by [CyberScoop](https://cyberscoop.com/microsoft-patch-tuesday-april-2026/).

This follows [The Machine Herald's earlier coverage](/article/2026-03/15-microsoft-march-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-84-vulnerabilities-including-two-public-zero-days-and-a-critical-ai-discovered-flaw) of Microsoft's March 2026 release, which patched 84 flaws — roughly half the size of this month's haul.

## What We Know

**SharePoint spoofing under active attack.** The most urgent fix is CVE-2026-32201, a spoofing vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint Server rated CVSS 6.5. [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-april-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-167-flaws-2-zero-days/) reports that the flaw stems from "improper input validation in Microsoft Office SharePoint" and allows "an unauthorized attacker to perform spoofing over a network," letting attackers view sensitive information and modify disclosed content. Mike Walters of Action1 told [Krebs on Security](https://krebsonsecurity.com/2026/04/patch-tuesday-april-2026-edition/) the vulnerability "can enable phishing attacks, unauthorized data manipulation, or social engineering campaigns."

**Defender privilege escalation dubbed "BlueHammer."** The second zero-day, CVE-2026-33825, is an elevation-of-privilege vulnerability in the Microsoft Defender anti-malware platform. [Krebs on Security](https://krebsonsecurity.com/2026/04/patch-tuesday-april-2026-edition/) reports that the bug — nicknamed "BlueHammer" — had public exploit code released before the patch. [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-april-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-167-flaws-2-zero-days/) notes the fix ships in Microsoft Defender Antimalware Platform version 4.18.26050.3011, and that successful exploitation grants SYSTEM-level privileges on the affected machine.

**Eight critical flaws, mostly remote code execution.** Beyond the two zero-days, [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-april-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-167-flaws-2-zero-days/) reports Microsoft rated eight vulnerabilities Critical — seven remote code execution bugs and one denial of service — and breaks the full set down as 93 elevation of privilege, 20 remote code execution, 21 information disclosure, 13 security feature bypass, 10 denial of service, and 9 spoofing flaws. Multiple critical RCEs affect Microsoft Office, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, exploitable via the preview pane or by opening a malicious document.

**Collateral patches from other vendors.** [Krebs on Security](https://krebsonsecurity.com/2026/04/patch-tuesday-april-2026-edition/) notes that the same week, Google Chrome shipped a fix for its fourth zero-day of 2026, and Adobe issued an emergency update for Adobe Reader covering CVE-2026-34621, a remote code execution flaw that had been actively exploited since November 2025. Adam Barnett of Rapid7 told [Krebs on Security](https://krebsonsecurity.com/2026/04/patch-tuesday-april-2026-edition/) that the broader spike includes "nearly 60 browser vulnerabilities" and reflects "ever-expanding AI capabilities" in vulnerability discovery.

## What We Don't Know

Microsoft has not publicly attributed the SharePoint exploitation to a specific threat actor or disclosed how many organizations have been targeted. The company also has not said when in-the-wild exploitation of CVE-2026-32201 began or whether the activity is linked to a known espionage or ransomware campaign. The identity of the researchers who reported the SharePoint flaw is not listed in Microsoft's advisory, according to [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-april-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-167-flaws-2-zero-days/). The Defender bug is credited to Zen Dodd and Yuanpei XU (HUST with Diffract) in the same report.

It is also unclear how much of April's record-size release reflects a genuine surge in newly discovered bugs versus a backlog working its way through Microsoft's Security Response Center queue. ZDI's Childs framed the tripling of submissions as a triage problem, per [CyberScoop](https://cyberscoop.com/microsoft-patch-tuesday-april-2026/), but did not provide a breakdown of how many April CVEs originated from AI-assisted research.

## Guidance

Administrators running on-premises SharePoint Server should prioritize the April update given confirmed in-the-wild exploitation. Endpoints should verify that Microsoft Defender has updated to version 4.18.26050.3011 or later to close the BlueHammer privilege escalation path. [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-april-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-167-flaws-2-zero-days/) additionally flags the Office preview-pane RCE chain as a priority for organizations that routinely receive external attachments.
